_comment: Static Big-O reference for the Legacy non-generic family (SPEC §3/§5.B). Keyed by <Collection>_<Op>, matching gen_report.py name parsing. amortized/avg where relevant. SPACE notes call out BOXING heap overhead: every value-type element (int/ValStruct) AND every int key stored in these object-typed containers is boxed into a separate heap object (~object header + payload), so a build of N value elems carries ~N boxed-object allocations ON TOP OF the backing-array growth. RefElem is already a reference -> no boxing.

ArrayList_Add.space: O(1) amortized array growth + 1 boxed heap obj per value elem (int/val); ref = no box

ArrayList_Insert0.space: O(1) array growth amortized + 1 boxed heap obj per value elem; head shift moves refs, no extra alloc

ArrayList_RemoveAt.space: O(1) (no alloc; boxed objs become garbage as removed)

ArrayList_Set.space: O(1) per write, but +1 NEW boxed heap obj per value-elem assignment (int/val); ref = no box

ArrayList_Index.space: O(1) (returns stored boxed reference; no new box on read)

ArrayList_Contains.space: O(1) (linear scan; probe value boxed once per call for int/val)

ArrayList_Iterate.space: O(1) (enumerator yields stored object refs; no per-elem box)

Hashtable_Add.space: O(1) avg bucket growth + boxed int KEY heap obj per entry + boxed value heap obj for int/val (ref value = no box)

Hashtable_Remove.space: O(1) (key probe boxed once per call; entry objs become garbage)

Hashtable_Set.space: O(1), but key probe boxed + NEW boxed value heap obj per value-elem write (int/val); ref = no value box

Hashtable_Get.space: O(1) (int key boxed once per ContainsKey/indexer lookup; value returned as stored ref, no new box)

Hashtable_Iterate.space: O(1) (foreach DictionaryEntry: entry struct boxed per step by the non-generic enumerator)

Queue_Enqueue.space: O(1) amortized ring-buffer growth + 1 boxed heap obj per value elem (int/val); ref = no box

Queue_Dequeue.space: O(1) (no alloc; boxed objs become garbage as dequeued)

Queue_Peek.space: O(1) (linear Contains; probe boxed once per call for int/val)

Queue_Iterate.space: O(1) (enumerator yields stored object refs; no per-elem box)

Stack_Push.space: O(1) amortized array growth + 1 boxed heap obj per value elem (int/val); ref = no box

Stack_Pop.space: O(1) (no alloc; boxed objs become garbage as popped)

Stack_Peek.space: O(1) (linear Contains; probe boxed once per call for int/val)

Stack_Iterate.space: O(1) (enumerator yields stored object refs; no per-elem box)

SortedList_Add.time: O(log n) search + O(n) shift; ascending keys -> O(1) amortized tail append (O(n) build)
SortedList_Add.space: O(1) amortized key/value array growth + boxed int KEY heap obj per entry + boxed value heap obj for int/val (ref value = no box)

SortedList_Remove.space: O(1) (key probe boxed once per call; entry objs become garbage)

SortedList_Set.space: O(1), but key probe boxed + NEW boxed value heap obj per value-elem write (int/val); ref = no value box

SortedList_ContainsKey.space: O(1) (int key boxed once per lookup)

SortedList_Iterate.space: O(1) (foreach DictionaryEntry: entry struct boxed per step by the non-generic enumerator)

BitArray_Set.space: O(1) (packed 32 bits/int; pure bit write, NO boxing)

BitArray_Get.space: O(1) (pure bit read, NO boxing; bool returned by value)

BitArray_Iterate.space: O(1) live, but non-generic enumerator boxes each bool per step
